input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS development)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)
- Install Xcode Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install`
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install components
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release — check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) (pick the latest LTS or STS with MAUI support)
- Use the macOS installer (`.pkg`) or install via Homebrew: `brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet --list-sdks` after install

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- Verify: `dotnet workload list` (should show `maui`)

## 4. Install Android dependencies

The MAUI workload pulls Android SDK/NDK automatically, but you need:

- **JDK 17** — `brew install --cask microsoft-openjdk` (Microsoft's recommended build)
- Android SDK/emulator are managed by the workload, but for emulator images:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```

## 5. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit

- **Source:** [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com)
- Extensions: **C# Dev Kit**, **.NET MAUI** extension

Or use **Visual Studio for Mac's successor workflow** (VS Code is now the recommended macOS IDE).

## 6. Verify the full setup

```bash
# Check .NET
dotnet --info

# Check workloads
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 7. Version compatibility cheat sheet

| Component | How to find required version |
|-----------|------------------------------|
| .NET SDK | [dotnet.microsoft.com/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) |
| Xcode | MAUI release notes per .NET version |
| Android API | Defined in `.csproj` `TargetFramework` |
| JDK | Must be 17; set `JAVA_HOME` |

## Key tips

- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- If builds fail with iOS signing errors, open **Keychain Access** and verify your Apple Developer certificate, or use `dotnet build -p:_BundlerDebug=true` for diagnostics.
- For physical iOS devices, configure signing in the `.csproj` with your Team ID.
